descriptivenote.p: In English and French, with additional materials in Chinese, Spanish, and Portuguese.

did.abstract: The collection consists of material created and accumulated by Ron Padgett in the course of his work as a poet, translator, and editor. Material includes extensive correspondence with other poets and writers; manuscripts for Padgett's books of poetry and prose, as well as some juvenilia and early writings; manuscripts for Padgett's English translations of works by French-language poets including Guillaume Apollinaire, Blaise Cendrars, Valery Larbaud, Paul Morand, and Pierre Reverdy; manuscripts of collected letters and writings by others arranged and edited by Padgett, including works by Joe Brainard, Ted Berrigan, and Frank O'Hara; and manuscripts for writings by others, including filmmaker Jim Jarmusch. Also included are "bokes," the term coined by Padgett to describe the "little unique editions" Ted Berrigan and he made for each other beginning in the 1960s, which encompass dozens of unique handmade works by Tom Clark, Anne Waldman, Patricia Padgett, Bill Berkson, George Schneeman, Michael Brownstein, Larry Fagin, Tom Veitch, Aram Saroyan, Steve Carey, and Lewis Warsh, among others. Also found are notebooks, including numerous travel diaries, project notebooks, and personal journals; appointment books for the years 1971 to 2008; as well as teaching files; student papers; research material; printed material; and personal papers. Also included is a collection of the writings and correspondence of Padgett's friend, gay artist and author Joe Brainard, as well as files for White Dove Review , edited by Padgett, along with Dick Gallup and Joe Brainard, while they were high school students in Tulsa, and Full Court Press , edited and published by Padgett, Joan Simon, and Anne Waldman from 1974 to 1989.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Boxes 89, 134-135: (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 136-142 (audiovisual media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 143 (student records): Restricted until 2089. For further information, consult appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books, Inc. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2009, 2019.

bioghist.head: Ron Padgett
bioghist.p: Ron Padgett was born in Tulsa, Oklahoma in 1942. He began writing at the age of 13 and started a little magazine in high school called the White Dove Review with friends Dick Gallup and Joe Brainard. In its five issues, the magazine published Allen Ginsberg, Jack Kerouac, Robert Creeley, LeRoi Jones (now Amiri Baraka), Ted Berrigan, and others.

In 1960, he moved to New York, where he attended Columbia College and studied with Kenneth Koch and Lionel Trilling. Padgett later spent a year in Paris on a Fulbright fellowship where he studied French literature.

His first collection of poems, Bean Spasms , written with Ted Berrigan, was published in 1967. Since then, he has published several books of poetry, including How to Be Perfect (Coffee House Press, 2007), You Never Know (2002), Poems I Guess I Wrote (2001), New & Selected Poems (1995), The Big Something (1990), Triangles in the Afternoon (1979), and Great Balls of Fire (1969).

He has also published a volume of selected prose titled Blood Work (1993), as well as translations of Blaise Cendrars' Complete Poems (1992), Pierre Cabanne's Dialogues with Marcel Duchamp (1971), and Guillaume Apollinaire's The Poet Assassinated (1968).

Padgett was the editor-in-chief of World Poets , a three-volume reference book (Scribner, 2000). He served as director of the St. Mark's Poetry Project from 1978 to 1980 and then was the publications director of Teachers & Writers Collaborative for twenty years. He was elected as a Chancellor of the Academy of American Poets in 2008. He lives in New York City.
Biographical note is drawn from Poets.org. More information about Padgett can be found on his website.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Ron Padgett Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The collection was received by the library in 157 boxes, which were organized and described by the dealer in ten sections, according to where the material originated and/or was stored. Six sections of the dealer list described material stored in Ron Padgett's apartment (in the living room, the closet, or above the bed); three sections described material stored in Manhattan Ministorage; and one section described material originating from Vermont, where Padgett spent summers. Similar types of material (for instance, correspondence and writings) could be found in all ten sections.

The collection was processed to a preliminary level in 2009 shortly after it was acquired. Preliminary processing included reboxing and refoldering all material, and organizing material at the box-level into series based on activity or format. Material within boxes and folders have not been sorted. Original section and box numbers have been recorded on each folder as part of rehousing so that it's possible to reconstruct the original physical groupings, or sections, of material.

Information included in the Description of the Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn almost entirely from the dealer description and list. This information has not been verified by the library, and is included in the finding aid in order to provide more detail about the context and content of the collection. Titles in brackets were supplied by the archivist. In order to compensate for the lack of sorting within boxes and folders, the list of correspondents for each box or file has, when available, been incorporated into the contents list. Names are repeated throughout the finding aid and researchers will have to look in numerous boxes to locate all the correspondence of a particular individual; however, by doing a key word search, it will be possible for researchers to identify where such correspondence is located.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Other material by or relating to Ron Padgett can be found by searching the Uncataloged Acquisitions Database and Orbis (Yale's Online Catalog).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of material created and accumulated by Ron Padgett in the course of his work as a poet, translator, and editor. Material includes extensive correspondence with other poets and writers; manuscripts for Padgett's books of poetry and prose, as well as some juvenilia and early writings; manuscripts for Padgett's English translations of works by French-language poets including Guillaume Apollinaire, Blaise Cendrars, Valery Larbaud, Paul Morand, and Pierre Reverdy; manuscripts of collected letters and writings by others arranged and edited by Padgett, including works by Joe Brainard, Ted Berrigan, and Frank O'Hara; and manuscripts for writings by others, including filmmaker Jim Jarmusch. Also included are "bokes," the term coined by Padgett to describe the "little unique editions" Ted Berrigan and he made for each other beginning in the 1960s, which encompass dozens of unique handmade works by Tom Clark, Anne Waldman, Patricia Padgett, Bill Berkson, George Schneeman, Michael Brownstein, Larry Fagin, Tom Veitch, Aram Saroyan, Steve Carey, and Lewis Warsh, among others. Also found are notebooks, including numerous travel diaries, project notebooks, and personal journals; appointment books for the years 1971 to 2008; as well as teaching files; student papers; research material; printed material; and personal papers. Also included is a collection of the writings and correspondence of Padgett's friend, gay artist and author Joe Brainard, as well as files for White Dove Review , edited by Padgett, along with Dick Gallup and Joe Brainard, while they were high school students in Tulsa, and Full Court Press , edited and published by Padgett, Joan Simon, and Anne Waldman from 1974 to 1989.

Correspondents include John Ashbery, Paul Auster, Glen Baxter, Bill Berkson, Ted Berrigan, Joe Brainard, Michael Brownstein, Rudy Burckhardt, Tom Clark, Clark Coolidge, Jim Dine, Kenward Elmslie, Clayton Eshleman, Larry Fagin, Kathleen Fraser, Dick Gallup, Lyn Hejinian, Susan Howe, Alex Katz, Kenneth Koch, Ann Lauterbach, Harry Mathews, Bernadette Mayer, Alice Notley, Maureen Owen, Tom Raworth, Charles Reznikov, Ed Sanders, Aram Saroyan, George Schneeman, James Schuyler, Tony Towle, Tom Veitch, Anne Waldman, Lewis Warsh, and Trevor Winkfield, among others. Among the highlights are a complete file of correspondence between Padgett and Joe Brainard from 1962 to 1993, including Padgett's letters to Brainard which were returned to Padgett upon Brainard's death in 1994; a complete file of correspondence between Padgett and Berrigan from 1959 to 1983; and extensive correspondence from James Schuyler, as well as correspondence from Schuyler to Joe Brainard, dating from 1966 to 1990.

The White Dove Review files consist of all known correspondence and manuscripts, including those of Allen Ginsberg, Jack Kerouac, e. e. cummings, Robert Creeley, LeRoi Jones, David Meltzer, and Ron Lowinsohn, among others, and provide documentation of the magazine that helped launch Padgett's career and that influenced many of the Second Generation New York School little magazines and presses.

The Full Court Press files consist of all known correspondence, manuscripts, proofs, production material, promotion files, and business records. Writers published by Full Court include Joe Brainard, William Burroughs, Edwin Denby, Larry Fagin, Allen Ginsberg, John Godfrey, Frank O'Hara, Phillipe Soupault (William Carlos Williams, translator), and Tom Veitch.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Issue #1 of Sesheta , originally filed in Box 68, was separated from the collection and cataloged in February 2020.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Ron Padgett Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is organized into two groupings: February 2009 acquisition, 1929-2008, and October 2019 acquisition, 1922-2019.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into seven series: Correspondence, Writings, Notebooks and Bokes, White Dove Review , Full Court Press , Teaching Files, and Personal and Professional Papers.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into broad groups according to location of origin or storage: Living Room Top Shelf; Manhattan Ministorage; and Vermont. Some outgoing correspondence is arranged as a group at the end. Outgoing correspondence can also be found intermingled with incoming correspondence in other groups.
Correspondence has not been arranged or sorted within boxes and folders.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Includes lists of correspondents from dealer list, when available. The best way to locate correspondence from a particular individual is to do a keyword search by name.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into seven series: I. Correspondence, 1954-2017. II. Writings, 1922-2018. III. Bokes and Artwork, 1929-2010. IV. Joe Brainard Papers, 1960-2017. V. Personal and Professional Papers, 1955-2019. VI. Computer and Audiovisual Media, 1953-2018.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This accession consists of additional material created and accumulated by Ron Padgett in the course of his work as a poet, translator, and editor. Material includes correspondence with other poets and writers; manuscripts for Padgett's books of poetry and prose, as well as some early writings; manuscripts for Padgett's English translations of works by French-language poets including Guillaume Apollinaire, Blaise Cendrars, Valery Larbaud, Paul Morand, and Pierre Reverdy; manuscripts of collected works edited by Padgett, including the writings and letters of Joe Brainard, Ted Berrigan, and Frank O'Hara; and manuscripts by others, including filmmaker Jim Jarmusch. Also included are "bokes," the term coined by Padgett to describe the "little unique editions" Ted Berrigan and he made for each other beginning in the 1960s, which encompass dozens of unique handmade works by Joe Brainard, Dick Gallup, Alice Notley, Patricia Padgett, George Schneeman, and Bill Berkson, among others. Also found are notebooks and personal journals; research material; printed material; personal papers; and a collection of the writings and correspondence of Padgett's friend, gay artist and author Joe Brainard.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of personal and professional correspondence of Ron Padgett and others, as well as materials enclosed with letters, such as writings, contracts, clippings, and photographs. Correspondents include John Ashbery, Bill Berkson, Ted Berrigan, Sandy Berrigan, Jim Dine, Kenward Elmslie, Larry Fagin, Jim Jarmusch, Kenneth Koch, Larry Rivers, David Shapiro, Anne Waldman, and Trevor Winkfield.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Alphabetical Correspondence, and Other Correspondence.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Additional correspondence can be found in Series II. Writings, Series IV. Joe Brainard Papers, and Series V. Personal and Professional Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of correspondence between Ron Padgett and his friends, colleagues, and publishers.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by correspondent with unidentified and unsorted correspondence filed at the end of the sequence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of correspondence between Ron Padgett and his friends, colleagues, and publishers. Materials are grouped by type of correspondence or location. "Vermont correspondence" refers to letters written and received at Padgett's home in Vermont.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by file title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains material related Ron Padgett's writings, as well as his works of translation, editorial projects, interviews, and writings of others.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five subseries: Poetry and Prose, Editorial Files, Translations, Interviews, and Writings of Others.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Additional writings can be found in Series I. Correspondence, Series III. Bokes and Artwork, and Series IV. Joe Brainard Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains drafts of Ron Padgett's writings and related materials, such as correspondence, reviews, graphic design documents, notes, and research materials. Translations of Padgett's works by others, sometimes with Padgett's contributions, are included in this series along with some smaller works of others translated by Padgett. Also included are collaborative works, such as scripts and associated material for Jim Jarmusch's film Paterson (2016), for which Padgett wrote the poetry of the titular character.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by file or work title, with writings identified by location, unsorted book reviews, and unsorted writings filed at the end of the sequence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains drafts of front and back matter, photocopies of writings and correspondence, and other materials related to collected works edited by Ron Padgett. Included are materials for Dear Sandy, Hello: Letters from Ted to Sandy Berrigan , The Letters of Frank O'Hara , and an exhibition on the works of George Schneeman at the New York City Poets House.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains drafts, original foreign language copies of works, and other documentation related to Ron Padgett's translation projects. These include his English translations of works by French-language writers such as Guillaume Apollonaire, Blaise Cendrars, Valery Larbaud, Paul Morand, and Pierre Reverdy, as well as several French translations of English-language works, and materials related to a collaborative translation of Chinese poet Yu Jian's Flash Cards with translator Wang Ping.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains materials assembled by Ron Padgett by and about artist and writer Joe Brainard in the course of his research and assembly of several books about Brainard, including The Collected Writings of Joe Brainard and The Nancy Book . Included are correspondence, drafts of writings, works of art, exhibition planning materials, and printed material.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four subseries: Correspondence, Writings and Artwork, Exhibitions, and Printed Material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains correspondence between Joe Brainard and others, including Ron Padgett, John Ashbery, Bill Berkson, Kenward Elmslie, Jon Galassi, David Lehman, Keith McDermott, Fairfield Porter, James Schuyler, and Tom Veitch. Most outgoing correspondence from Brainard consists of photocopies from archival collections held by institutions other than Yale University.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by name, with unsorted and unidentified correspondence filed at the end of the sequence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains works of art and drafts of writings by Joe Brainard, as well as materials related to collections of Brainard's work edited by Ron Padgett, including The Collected Writings of Joe Brainard , The Nancy Book , and Selected Writings .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains correspondence, financial records, ephemera, loan documentation, and other records related to the planning and installation of exhibitions of Joe Brainard's work. Ephemera for Brainard's first solo exhibition, "Joe Brainard: Assemblages and Collages" at Alan Gallery in 1966 are included, as well as materials for a retrospective travelling exhibition of Brainard's work in 2001.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically by exhibition date.

did.unittitle: "Joe Brainard: Assemblages and Collages," Alan Gallery, 1966 January, ephemera
"Joe Brainard: A Retrospective," Tibor de Nagy Gallery, 1997 March-April
Tibor de Nagy, other exhibitions, ephemera
"Joe's Room" event at the Michael Callen-Audre Lorde Community Health Center, correspondence and ephemera
Boulder Museum of Contemporary Art show, 2001 June-September, correspondence and planning material
PS1 Contemporary Arts Center, 2001 September-November, planning materials and photographs
Tibor de Nagy Gallery, 2001 October-November, ephemera and planning material
University of Nevada Las Vegas, 2002 January-February, ephemera and photographs
"Joe Brainard: Selected Works," Tibor de Nagy Gallery, 2004 January-February, ephemera and planning material
"Joe Brainard: Selected Works," Tibor de Nagy Gallery, 2004 January-February, installation photographs
"Joe Brainard: 36 Pictures and Some Works," Brazos Projects, Houston, planning material
"Joe Brainard, People of the World: Relax!" University at Buffalo Art Gallery, 2007 January-March, correspondence and planning material
"Joe Brainard: The Erotic Work," Tibor de Nagy Gallery, 2007 March-April, ephemera and planning material
"The Third Mind," Palais de Tokyo, Paris, 2007 September-December, correspondence and planning material
"Joe Brainard: Painting the Way I Wish I Could Talk," Tibor de Nagy Gallery, 2012 March-April, ephemera

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains printed material related to Joe Brainard's life and work, including periodicals, clippings, and ephemera.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by publication or file title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes materials related to Ron Padgett's education, personal life, involvement in professional organizations, awards, grants, residencies, finances, and speaking engagements.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Yearbooks and Memorabilia, Professional and Financial Papers, and Readings, Classes, and Conferences.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains Ron Padgett's middle and high school yearbooks, memorabilia from Padgett's time living in Lancaster, South Carolina, a class directory from Columbia University, notes and ephemera, merchandise from the film Paterson by Jim Jarmusch, and a baseball signed by Padgett and his friends from a game in 1968.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by file title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains material related to literary and financial awards, grant applications, residencies, apartment rentals, legal matters, and professional and literary organizations with which Ron Padgett was affiliated. Also included are financial records such as royalty statements and travel receipts.

arrangement.head: Arrangement

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of files on Ron Padgett's speaking engagements, including correspondence, financial records, writings, notes, contracts, and ephemera related to readings, classes, talks, and conferences.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically, with unsorted materials filed at the end of the sequence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of born-digital and audiovisual materials created or collected by Ron Padgett, including computer disks, one external hard-drive, floppy disks, zip disks, audiocassettes, videocassettes, and reels of 8mm film.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Computer Media, and Audiovisual Media.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 134-135 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 136-138 (audiocassettes): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 139-140 (videocassettes): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 141-142 (8mm film): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains analog audio and video media, such as audiocassettes, microcassettes, VHS tapes, and reels of 8mm film. Most recordings on 8mm film are duplicated on VHS, and some taped events are recorded in both audio and video format. A small amount of materials enclosed with audiovisual media and original boxes for media rehoused or reformatted by Ron Padgett are also included.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four subseries: Audiocassettes, Videocassettes, 8mm Film, and Enclosures.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of 244 audiocassettes and 6 microcassettes containing interviews, talks and readings, radio programs, and home recordings from Ron Padgett and his friends.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 136-138 (audiocassettes): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Transcripts of these recordings can be found in Series II. Writings, subseries Poetry and Prose.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of 35 VHS tapes containing Ron Padgett's home videos, taped talks and readings, and interviews. Also included are several tapes related to Padgett's friends and colleagues, including Joe Brainard and James Schuyler.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 139-140 (videocassettes): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of nine reels of film containing Ron Padgett's home videos and one reel containing three recordings about Joe Brainard. Included are thirty-five reels of home videos transferred from their original 3-inch reels onto five 7-inch reels.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title.

did.unittitle: "Ron Padgett home videos" [5 7-inch reels, transferred from 35 3-inch reels]

#1-8: 1. "Ron, Dick, TU, Lucille, Trixie, Edna, home interior, circa 1957 March" / 2. "Lauren, Sharon, Craig Bolon, Mary Gallup, Lucille, Dick, hamster, Dixie, Alma, et al, circa 1957 April" / 3. untitled, 1957 April / 4. "OKK and Ron at Philbrook, 1957 April" / 5. "[Hueys], James' Belly dance and miscellaneous Tulsa, 1957 July" / 6-7. "Arkansas Trip with Vaughans and Hueys, Lucille, rain storm, Summer 1957" / 8. "Dick, Ron, the MG, Mary Jane, 1958 May 3"

#9-16: 9. "Eureka Springs, Arkansas, hill climb, part 1, 1958 May" / 10. "Eureka Springs, Arkansas, hill climb, part 2" / 11. "Eureka Springs, Arkansas, hill climb, part 3 / Dog, roses, Lucille, MG, 1958 May" / 12. "Levelland, TX, visit to Rene and James's, 1958 May" / 13. "Bad storm, kids, cat, home, Berry, John Gallup, Dick, 1958 August" / 14. "Mt. Nebo sports car race / Bill Martin, MG, Edna, some kid, 1958 August" / 15. "Ron bowling, winter, 2 beatniks (Dick and Ron), 1958 December" / 16. untitled, circa 1959-1960

#17-24: 17. "Pat, neighborhood, swimming, Summer 1962 or 1963" / 18. "Pat, Congetta, Wayne, Dean, Bernie, Tessie, Ron and, at end, Joe, Summer 1962" / 19. scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains letters, notes, and clippings enclosed with audiovisual media and original boxes for media that were rehoused or reformatted by Padgett.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Materials are open for research.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Audiocassettes originally accompanying these materials can be found in the subseries "Audiocassettes."
